content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

October 3, 2006

Charles H. Bell, Jr.
Bell, McAndrews & Hiltachk, LLP
455 Capitol Mall, Suite 801
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-06-162

Dear Mr. Bell:

This letter is written notification that we have withdrawn the above-captioned
request for advice relating to the "revolving door" provisions of the Political Reform
Act.¹ As of this date, you have not provided the information requested in our September
13, 2006, correspondence.

If you are able to obtain the requested information in the future, please submit
another inquiry.

Should you have any questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

John W. Wallace
Acting General Counsel

I:\Advice Ltrs\06-162w

¹ Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-
18997 of the California Code of Regulations.

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

September 13, 2006

Charles H. Bell, Jr.
Bell, McAndrews & Hiltachk, LLP
455 Capitol Mall, Suite 801
Sacramento, California 95814

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 06-162

Dear Mr. Bell:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the revolving
door provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ The Commission does
not advise with respect to past conduct. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A), copy
enclosed.) Therefore, we are declining to advise on your second question which
appears to involve conduct that has already occurred.

With respect to your first question, the Commission may only provide
formal written advice to someone whose duties are in question under the Act or to
their authorized representation. (Government Code section 83114(b).)

Regulation 18329(b)(2) specifies:

"(2) Requests for formal written advice will not be acted
upon unless the following requirements are met:

"(A) The name, title or position, and mailing address of the
person whose duties are in question are provided. In addition, if
the request is submitted by an authorized representative, it shall
contain a specific statement that such authorization has been
made."

Additionally, your inquiry is hypothetical, which under regulation
18329(b)(8)(D), is a circumstance in which the Fair Political Practices
Commission may decline to provide written advice.

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2,
sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. 06-162
Page No. 2

Thus, in order to proceed with the letter, we will need the name of the
person whose duties are in question. If we do not receive this information by
September 27, 2006, we will consider your request to be withdrawn.

Should you have any additional questions on this matter, please contact
the Commission at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division

Enclosure
JWW:rd
I:\AdviceLtrs\06-162

sections.conclusion: This letter is written notification that we have withdrawn the above-captioned request for advice relating to the "revolving door" provisions of the Political Reform Act. As of this date, you have not provided the information requested in our September 13, 2006, correspondence.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asked about the revolving door provisions of the Political Reform Act, including both a prospective question about future conduct and a retrospective question about past conduct.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC withdrew the advice request because the requestor failed to provide required information (the name of the person whose duties are in question) by the September 27, 2006 deadline specified in the prior letter.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a two-part document containing both the September 13, 2006 declination letter (declining to advise on past conduct and requesting additional information) and the October 3, 2006 withdrawal letter (confirming withdrawal due to non-response). The withdrawal letter is the primary document being analyzed here.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requestor asked about the revolving door provisions of the Political Reform Act, including both a prospective question about future conduct and a retrospective question about past conduct.

CONCLUSION: This letter is written notification that we have withdrawn the above-captioned request for advice relating to the "revolving door" provisions of the Political Reform Act. As of this date, you have not provided the information requested in our September 13, 2006, correspondence.

embedding.summary: The FPPC withdrew an advice request regarding the Political Reform Act's revolving door provisions due to the requestor's failure to timely provide required information about the person whose duties were in question.
